title: Chocolate Truffle Cookies with Crackly Crust
ingredients: 3 tablespoons plus 1 teaspoon unsweetened cocoa powder
1 pound plus 4 ounces bittersweet chocolate, chopped
1/2 cup plus 2 tablespoons unsalted butter, softened
instructions: In a bowl, sift together flour, cocoa and baking powder.
Stir in the salt and set aside.

Place chopped chocolate in a heatproof bowl over a saucepan of very hot but not boiling water (the bottom of the bowl should not touch the water), stirring occasionally until chocolate is melted and smooth.
Remove bowl from water and allow to cool 5 to 10 minutes.
Combine butter and sugar in the bowl of an electric mixer with the paddle attachment and cream on medium speed until well combined.
Add eggs, one at a time, mixing on medium speed until eggs are incorporated.
Increase speed to high and beat for a few minutes until mixture is very light, creamy, and pale in color, scraping bowl down as needed.
Add melted chocolate and vanilla extract and mix just until combined.
Remove bowl from mixer and fold in the dry ingredients using a rubber spatula.

Scoop cookies onto parchment-lined baking sheets, placing them about 2 inches apart using a 2-ounce ice cream scoop packed about three-quarters full, or use a scant 1/4 cup or 1 1/2 ounces of cookie dough for each cookie.
Flatten each mound of dough slightly with your hand.
Put cookies in the oven soon after scooping and bake until they are evenly cracked all over the tops and softly set, 14 to 16 minutes, rotating pan about halfway through the baking time.
If baking multiple pans simultaneously, switch them between the racks halfway through.
Remove pans from the oven and cool on a wire rack.
Allow cookies to cool completely before removing them from the baking sheets with a metal spatula.
notes: Makes about 30 cookies that are 3 1/2 inches in diameter.
The batter is very soft at first but starts firming up quickly as it sits, which will make it more difficult to portion, so scoop cookies as soon as you finish making the batter.
You can use a dampened hand when flattening cookies because the dough is sticky.
If baking in batches, don't refrigerate the scooped dough but leave them at room temperature, as these cookies will not spread properly if the dough is chilled first.
Cookies stick to the parchment paper slightly, but you can scrape them off with a sturdy metal spatula easily.
